name: Crustade of Chicken and Pigeon
recipeInstructions:
1. Roll out 8 oz of the pastry and line an 8-inch flan dish; blind bake.
2. Put the pigeon in a pot with the stock, wine, pepper, and cloves and cook very slowly for an hour. Add the chicken and continue to cook for a further 45 minutes or until the meat of both birds is really tender. Meanwhile, cook the mushrooms lightly in the butter.
3. Remove the birds from the stock and bone them. Cut the flesh into small pieces, mix it with the mushrooms and raisins, and spread over the base of the flan case.
4. Beat the eggs with a fork and season with salt, pepper, and ginger. Add 1 cup of the cooking juices and pour over the meat in the flan case. If you want to have a lid, roll out the remaining pastry and cover the flan.
5. Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 25 minutes if uncovered, or 35 minutes if covered. Serve warm with a good green salad.
For a more modern flavor, double the chicken, leave out the pigeon, and substitute 1 oz chopped fried bacon for the raisins.
